name: Romance Novels
description: Wuthering Heights to 50 Shades — the love stories that define the genre.

questions.q: Who wrote the '50 Shades of Grey' trilogy?

source.notes: Originally a Twilight fanfic. First published 2011.

questions.q: Who wrote 'Pride and Prejudice'?
Who is the famously brooding hero of 'Wuthering Heights'?
Who is the iconic male lead in Jane Austen's 'Pride and Prejudice'?

source.notes: Played by Colin Firth (1995 BBC) and Matthew Macfadyen (2005 film).

questions.q: Who wrote 'Rebecca' (1938)?
What romance publishing house was founded in London in 1908?
Which Stephenie Meyer character is human protagonist Bella Swan's father?
Who wrote 'Outlander'?
Who is widely credited as the highest-selling romance author of all time, with an estimated 750 million+ books sold?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: changed correct answer to Cartland (~750M-1B copies, widely credited as the all-time top-selling romance author) and replaced 'Both' option with Nicholas Sparks.

questions.q: Which Charles Dickens novel features Pip's love for Estella?
Who wrote the novel 'The Notebook' (1996)?

source.notes: Adapted into 2004 film with Ryan Gosling and Rachel McAdams.

questions.q: Which 1936 novel by Margaret Mitchell features Scarlett O'Hara and Rhett Butler?
Which Charlotte Brontë novel features the orphan Jane and the brooding Mr. Rochester?
Who wrote 'The Bridges of Madison County' (1992)?

source.notes: Adapted to 1995 film with Meryl Streep and Clint Eastwood.

questions.q: Which contemporary romance series by Colleen Hoover became a TikTok phenomenon?

source.notes: Published 2016. Film starring Blake Lively + Justin Baldoni released August 2024.

questions.q: Who wrote 'Red, White & Royal Blue', adapted into a 2023 Amazon film?
Which Sylvia Day series shares similarities with 50 Shades?
Which Julia Quinn series became a Netflix sensation in 2020?

source.notes: First book published 2000. Series premiered December 2020.

questions.q: In Jane Austen's 'Pride and Prejudice', what is the name of the witty, independent-minded heroine pursued by Mr. Darcy?
The hugely popular 'Twilight' saga, centered on a romance between a human girl and a vampire, was written by which author?
In the 'Twilight' series, what is the name of the vampire who falls in love with Bella Swan?
Which 1847 Emily Brontë novel tells the doomed love story of Catherine Earnshaw and Heathcliff on the Yorkshire moors?
Nicholas Sparks's romance novel 'The Notebook' was adapted into a 2004 film starring Ryan Gosling and which actress?
In Margaret Mitchell's 'Gone with the Wind', what is the name of Scarlett O'Hara's beloved family plantation?
Which color is famously referenced in the title of E. L. James's bestselling erotic romance trilogy starring Christian Grey?
Diana Gabaldon's 'Outlander' follows nurse Claire Randall, who travels back in time to 18th-century Scotland and falls for which Highlander?
Daphne du Maurier's 1938 novel 'Rebecca' is set at which grand Cornish estate haunted by the memory of the first Mrs. de Winter?
Julia Quinn's 'Bridgerton' novels center on a London family whose eight children are all named in alphabetical order. What is the name of the eldest son?
Which Colleen Hoover novel, a sequel to 'It Ends with Us', was published in 2022 and follows the character Atlas Corrigan?
The famous opening line 'It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife' belongs to which novel?
In Charlotte Brontë's 'Jane Eyre', what dark secret is hidden in the attic of Mr. Rochester's Thornfield Hall?
Author Nora Roberts has published a long-running futuristic romantic-suspense series featuring NYPSD detective Eve Dallas under which pen name?
Which 1991 Diana Gabaldon novel launched the 'Outlander' book series?
Sarah J. Maas's bestselling 'A Court of Thorns and Roses' fantasy-romance series begins with the human huntress Feyre being taken to a magical realm by a lord of which kind of creatures?
Georgette Heyer is widely credited with essentially inventing which subgenre of historical romance, set during an early-19th-century English period?
Kathleen Woodiwiss's 1972 novel 'The Flame and the Flower' is often credited with launching which type of mass-market romance?
Which professional organization, founded in 1980 to support romance writers, presented the annual RITA Awards before renaming them the Vivian Awards?
In Outlander lore, Claire passes through a circle of standing stones to travel through time. By what name is this fictional stone circle known in the novels?
Which prolific author, sometimes called the 'Queen of Romance' before Barbara Cartland, wrote 'The Sheik' (1919), the desert romance later filmed with Rudolph Valentino?
Loretta Chase's acclaimed 1995 Regency romance, frequently topping 'best of' lists, pairs the rakish Marquess of Dain with Jessica Trent and is titled what?
Author Eloisa James, known for her Regency romances, is the pen name of a Shakespeare professor who is the daughter of which acclaimed poet?
In Julia Quinn's Bridgerton novels, the anonymous gossip columnist who scandalizes high society publishes under what pseudonym?
Beverly Jenkins is best known as a pioneering author of historical romances featuring which group of central characters, a then-underrepresented focus in the genre?
Helen Fielding's 1996 comic romance 'Bridget Jones's Diary' names its brooding love interest after the hero of a Jane Austen classic. What is that character's surname?
Lisa Kleypas's beloved Victorian-set romance series following the Hathaway siblings begins with which 2007 novel introducing Amelia Hathaway and Cam Rohan?
